query:
You are working on the Kotlin/kotlinx.coroutines repository.
The codebase is at version 1.6 (commit: 67e21b24).

## Issue to Resolve

CoroutineScope.future() may trigger global exception handler on cancellation
### Expected behavior

`future` coroutine builder delivers all errors via the returned object, just like `async`.

### Actual behavior

`future` coroutine builder can suddenly trigger global exception handler that may crash the application.

Sample code:
```kotlin
fun main() {
    val future = GlobalScope.future {
        error("foo")
    }
    future.cancel(true)
    Thread.sleep(100)
}
```

Result:
`Exception in thread "DefaultDispatcher-worker-1" java.lang.IllegalStateException: foo`

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

I believe `CompletableFutureCorotine` should not attempt to handle the exception in any other way than completing the returned `CompletableFuture`. For now, [this is not the case](https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx.coroutines/blob/d88a8c227a295f91251960c8fe6b21a02ddf704b/integration/kotlinx-coroutines-jdk8/src/future/Future.kt#L63).

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `kotlinx-coroutines-core/jdk8/src/future/Future.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

content.text:
After analyzing `Future.kt`, here is the root cause and the targeted fix:

## Analysis

- The current design of `CompletableFutureCoroutine` calls `handleCoroutineException` in `onCancelled` when the exception can't be delivered via the returned `CompletableFuture` (line 61).
- This can cause errors to propagate to the global coroutine exception handler, crashing the application on cancellation if the error can't be set on the `CompletableFuture`.

### Desired Behavior

- All exceptions in `CoroutineScope.future { ... }` must be delivered solely via the resulting `CompletableFuture`. No error should leak to the global handler (inconsistent with `async`).

## Targeted Fix

- Remove (or change) the call to `handleCoroutineException` in `onCancelled`. Instead, complete the future exceptionally and silence any further propagation, regardless of handled status.

## Minimal Change

- The following code block needs to be changed:
  ```kotlin
  override fun onCancelled(cause: Throwable, handled: Boolean) {
      if (!future.completeExceptionally(cause) && !handled) {
          // prevents loss of exception that was not handled by parent & could not be set to CompletableFuture
          handleCoroutineException(context, cause)
      }
  }
  ```
- Update it so that all exceptions are only set on the `CompletableFuture` and not sent to the global handler.

I will now implement this change.

content.text:
The fix is complete:

- Now, any exception from the coroutine in `future` is delivered only via the returned `CompletableFuture`, preventing propagation to the global exception handler.
- This matches the behavior of `async`, fully addressing the reported issue.
- No linter errors were introduced and no other code was changed, keeping the change minimal and safe.

You can proceed with testing or further review—the problem described has been resolved.
